author: mruberry
text: Tracker: Jiterator improvements

author: mruberry
text:
Jiterator TODOs:

* Support float16 and bfloat16 (can be tested by jiterating i0) [Low precision support for jiterator #70157](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/70157)
* Support complex64 and complex128 (can be tested by jiterating tanh backward or sgn) [Add complex support for Jiterator, port sinc to Jiterator #71577](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/71577)
* Support scalar inputs (can be testing by jiterating maximum for integer inputs) [Enable cpu scalar arguments for jiterator #69861](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/69861)
* Caching mechanism to be able to reuse compiled kernels in the different processes (e.g. on multiple runs of the same script)
* if more popular and perf sensitive operations are moved to jiterator, we can invest in kernel improvements, e.g. faster float <-> low precision conversion needed for amp, faster broadcast addition etc.
* support for functors that have state. Currently we are calling functions, thus cannot support functors that require runtime state (as opposed to the constants that can be hardcoded at compile time), such as `threshold` or addition/subtraction with `alpha`

cc [@ezyang](https://github.com/ezyang) [@gchanan](https://github.com/gchanan) [@zou3519](https://github.com/zou3519) [@bdhirsh](https://github.com/bdhirsh) [@jbschlosser](https://github.com/jbschlosser) [@ngimel](https://github.com/ngimel)

author: ezyang
text:
A suggestion for another improvement: at least internally at FB, which should be able to collect telemetry about how popular particular variants of kernels are. It would be nice to somehow work this into the "jiterate or no jiterate" decision in a way that makes it easier for people to see (e.g., maintaining a list of "here is what we think should be jiterated next").

For bonus points, figure out how to make this an opt in telemetry system for open source users.
